perturbation.original_text: The Distributor will be entitled to purchase certain Lucid products and resell them to End User customers within the following market segments:- hospital and medical research centres, including but not limited to dermatology, pathology, plastic surgery and mohs surgery departments, clinical research centres, small animal research facilities, cosmetic and cosmeceutical companies.
perturbation.changed_text: The Reseller will be entitled to purchase certain Lucid products and resell them to End User clients within the following market segments:- hospital and medical research centres, including but not limited to dermatology, pathology, plastic surgery and mohs surgery departments, clinical research centres, small animal research facilities, cosmetic and cosmeceutical companies.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Distributor' is a key element in the contract. By changing it to 'Reseller' in this section, but retaining 'Distributor' elsewhere, creates confusion about whether the entity responsible for reselling is the same entity granted exclusive distribution rights. This in-text contradiction affects the understanding of the entire agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Distributor will use his best efforts to actively promote and sell the Product to all relevant End Users within all relevant market segments within the Territory.
perturbation.justification: The original text states 'The Distributor will be entitled to purchase...' while the changed text replaces 'Distributor' with 'Reseller'. The contradicted text refers to 'The Distributor' promoting and selling the product. This creates a direct conflict because the contract now uses two different terms, 'Distributor' and 'Reseller', to describe the entity responsible for selling the product, leading to uncertainty about who is responsible for the obligations stated in the contradicted text. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Distributor agrees to purchase demonstration products as new products are released subject to special pricing. The Distributor agrees to use the demonstration equipment for the duties as noted in 5 A, B, C, D as above and not to re-sell this demonstration equipment to End Users.
perturbation.changed_text: The Sales Agent agrees to purchase demonstration products as new products are released subject to special pricing. The Distributor agrees to use the demonstration equipment for the duties as noted in 5 A, B, C, D as above and not to re-sell this demonstration equipment to End Users.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Distributor' to 'Sales Agent' only in this section creates a contradiction. While 'Distributor' is used in the subsequent sentence of the same section, as well as in other sections, it is unclear if the Sales Agent is actually the Distributor or some other entity. This affects the duties and right of the Distributor, as different from the Sales agent
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Sales Agent agrees to purchase demonstration products as new products are released subject to special pricing. The Distributor agrees to use the demonstration equipment for the duties as noted in 5 A, B, C, D as above and not to re-sell this demonstration equipment to End Users.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Distributor' with 'Sales Agent' in one instance while retaining 'Distributor' in the immediately subsequent sentence. This creates ambiguity and a potential contradiction because it is unclear whether the 'Sales Agent' and 'Distributor' are the same entity or separate entities. This is an instance of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction, where the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations. The contradicted text reveals the potential conflicting obligation because it is unclear if the 'Sales agent' can also be the 'Distributor'. This contradicts the original meaning and introduces uncertainty regarding the roles and responsibilities defined elsewhere in the contract for the Distributor.

perturbation.original_text: Lucid will supply to the Distributor, free of charge, images of skin in vivo. Lucid will make every reasonable effort to supply images of skin to the Distributor, which are required by the Distributor for a particular End User application.
perturbation.changed_text: Lucid will supply to the Partner, free of charge, images of skin in vivo. Lucid will make every reasonable effort to supply images of skin to the Distributor, which are required by the Distributor for a particular End User application.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Distributor' to 'Partner' only in this section creates ambiguity. Are 'Partner' and 'Distributor' supposed to be the same entities? This creates an in-text contradiction that makes obligations uncertain, specifically which entity Lucid has to make images of skin in vivo available to.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Lucid will supply to the Partner, free of charge, images of skin in vivo. Lucid will make every reasonable effort to supply images of skin to the Distributor, which are required by the Distributor for a particular End User application.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces 'Partner' where previously 'Distributor' was consistently used in the original text and contradicted text, which is the same, creating ambiguity as to which entity Lucid is obligated to supply images to. The perturbation explanation clearly states the ambiguity of the 'Partner' and 'Distributor' terms, leading to conflicting obligations, which fits the definition of Misaligned Terminology. This contradiction makes the contract unenforceable with respect to who Lucid is obligated to.
